channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Stellenbosch FC fights to stay relevant in PSL top eight
item.description: Stellenbosch FC’s bid to secure a spot in the top eight of the Betway Premiership is hanging by a thread heading into the final weekend of the season. Despite a recent win over Orbit College FC at the...

item.title: How Nigerians Celebrate Eid: Traditions, Food, and Family
item.description: For Nigerians, Eid no be just ordinary festival; e dey carry deep meaning wey touch sacrifice, generosity, and family togetherness. From early morning, families dey rush prepare their homes so everyth...

item.title: Tanzanian Government Strengthens Oversight of Mining Activities in Katavi
item.description: Tanzania’s Mining Minister, Elias Kwandikwa, recently visited Katavi Region to personally check how mining operations are going, showing the government’s commitment to proper regulation and oversight....

item.title: LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight supports two-player local co-op
item.description: LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, the latest release from TT Games, offers players a new action-adventure set in Gotham City. While LEGO games traditionally include multiplayer features, this ed...

item.title: Datu Rahman’s Journey of Redemption for Jalil in Episode 34 of Blood vs Duty
item.description: In Episode 34 of 'Blood vs Duty,' viewers witness the pivotal transformation of Datu Rahman, a central character in the series. Driven by loyalty and the deep bond he shares with Jalil, Datu Rahman un...

item.title: Donald Trump unsure about attending Donald Jr’s wedding in the Bahamas
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has indicated uncertainty over whether he will attend his son Donald Trump Jr’s upcoming wedding in the Bahamas. When asked about the event, Trump responded that the t...

item.title: Second Group of Australian IS-Linked Families Return from Syria Amid Rising Domestic Unemployment
item.description: A second group of Australian women and children with links to the Islamic State have departed a camp in north-eastern Syria to return home, according to the ABC. Buses were observed transporting the g...

item.title: APC shifts governorship and assembly primaries in Bauchi, Kwara, and Zamfara
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don announce say e go reschedule the Governorship Primary Elections for Bauchi and Kwara States go hold on Friday, May 22, 2026. E no stop there, the party also don...

item.title: ‘Deltarune’ soundtrack reimagined in rock guitar collection
item.description: The beloved soundtrack of Toby Fox’s episodic role-playing game ‘Deltarune’ has inspired a fresh reinterpretation in the form of a rock ‘n’ roll remix album. Musician Geoffrey Day has transformed four...

item.title: Libya Rolls Out Dollar Distribution Program to Citizens Through Banks
item.description: Libya imeshaanza kutoa Dola za Marekani moja kwa moja kwa wananchi wake kupitia benki za kibiashara. Hii ni hatua kubwa kwenye sera ya fedha ya nchi hiyo, ambayo inakabiliana na changamoto za uchumi n...

item.title: NBI Confirms Imminent Arrest of Senator Bato Dela Rosa Over ICC Warrant
item.description: National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag confirmed that the bureau is set to arrest Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa without delay. This follows an announcement by Justice Secreta...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Highlights Strong SA-Botswana Ties During State Visit
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa's State Visit to Botswana, hosted by President Duma Boko, was described as a 'coming together of brethren' reflecting the deep historical and cultural ties between South Afri...

item.title: Mozambican man sentenced to two life terms for rape and murder of 11-year-old girl in Kliptown
item.description: The Lenasia Regional Court has sentenced Jorge Tivane, a 21-year-old Mozambican national, to two life sentences for the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl in Kliptown in December 2022. Court proce...

item.title: UK net migration falls to lowest level since 2012, with asylum claims decreasing
item.description: Net migration to the United Kingdom fell to 171,000 in the year to March 2026, nearly half the figure recorded in 2024, marking the lowest level since 2012 excluding the Covid-19 pandemic, according t...

item.title: Queen Elizabeth II supported Prince Andrew's appointment as UK trade envoy
item.description: Newly released government files reveal that Queen Elizabeth II was keen for her son, Prince Andrew, to assume a trade envoy role in 2000. Documents obtained following a request by Sir Ed Davey, leader...

item.title: Hugo Broos Names 32-Player Preliminary Bafana Bafana Squad for 2026 World Cup
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has announced a 32-player preliminary squad for the 2026 Fifa World Cup, with Mamelodi Sundowns striker Iqraam Rayners making a notable return after being overlooked for...

item.title: GOP Congressmen Investigate Fraud in Medicaid Programs Affecting Immigrant Communities
item.description: Republican lawmakers, led by Rep. Brandon Gill of Texas, are investigating significant fraud and misuse of U.S. taxpayer funds within welfare programs, particularly Medicaid. During a Republican Study...

item.title: A Look at Katharine Ross's Career and Style in the 1960s
item.description: Katharine Ross, born on January 29, 1940, became one of the defining faces of American cinema in the 1960s. Known for her natural beauty and understated acting style, she transitioned from television ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Teachers’ Digital Skills to Improve Education
item.description: Tanzania government is making big moves to equip teachers with digital skills as part of efforts to improve education for all students across the country. Recently, in Dodoma, a four-day ICT Competenc...

item.title: A Retrospective Look at the TV Sitcom 'Bewitched' and Its Cultural Impact
item.description: “Bewitched,” created by Sol Saks and produced by Screen Gems, remains one of the most iconic American television shows, airing on ABC from 1964 to 1972. Starring Elizabeth Montgomery as Samantha Steph...
